note: HP-tuned + decay; beats d=128 baseline on both; deployed (int4 both-low-rank) targets met
+16.5k params for ZERO gain (ahead worse, imm tied); head resolution is not the lever
+14k FFN-capacity HURTS both -> d=32 model is DATA-limited at 100u, not capacity-limited
longer decay: ahead slightly worse, imm +0.00013 (< gate); 4-epoch decay is fine
epochs SATURATE at 15 on 100 users (ahead worse, imm better) -> motivates the more-DATA experiment

change: 1 epoch WS on 1500 users (1000-2499), WS-only (no decay)
note: DATA VARIETY beats repetition: beats the WS-15+4ep-decay champion on BOTH modes, WS-ONLY. Decay phase next. Confirms data-limited -> supports the 5k direction

change: t1500ws + 0.27-epoch cosine decay (1500 users; WS:decay ratio = champion's 15:4)
note: data-variety recipe (1 epoch on ~1500 users + decay) BEATS the 100u/15ep champion by +0.0051 ahead / +0.0038 imm -- the +0.0038 imm exceeds the entire iter0->old-champion loop. fp32; deployed (quant+low-rank) number pending. Confirms data-limited -> scale toward 5k (needs the smaller/faster model)

change: H=2/K=16 (n_heads=2, head_dim=16) via the new K<32 CUDA kernel; 1500u champion recipe (1ep WS 1000-2499 + 0.27ep decay)
note: SIZE/SPEED win (efficiency-budget gate, NOT the +0.0003 monotonic gate). Per-card WKV state HALVED 1088->576 floats; WS train 1.16x faster (1.182 vs 1.020 steps/s); accuracy PARITY (both modes within 0.0002, far inside +0.0015 budget). Still beats d=128 baseline +0.0106 ahead/+0.0053 imm. K<32 kernel parity-verified (test_k16_wkv.py). Next: re-tune HPs (smaller model + larger data). Rust deployed rev/s pending K<32 engine port. ckpt scratchpad/exp_h2k16/h2k16d_904.pth -> reference/champ_h2k16.safetensors

change: FIRST 5k champion run: train 1-5000 (2ep WS = 13108 steps + 0.5ep decay = 3277), quant-aware q72u with per-run LEARNABLE codebooks (cb exports at both seams), champion HPs (peak_lr 1e-3, warmup 200, wd 0.01, clip 0.25), MAX=110000, eval 5001-10000
note: PROMOTED to champion_5k.json (ckpt champ5kd_3277.pth + its learned cbs; WS step trace = the Wilcoxon prune ref). vs d=128 fp target on the same 5000 users: -0.010187 ahead / -0.013418 imm (paired one-sided Wilcoxon p=1.0 both -- behind the target, as expected for the 15x-smaller quant-aware model; the phase's job is closing this). Wall-clock: WS 5h00m (~1.36 s/step avg), decay 72 min, eval 2x-sharded 66 min (+14 min resume) -> ~7.0h clean pipeline. Two latent bugs hit+fixed: LEARN=1 optim resume param-group mismatch (f71f43b), per-user lmdb env leak in get_benchmark_info killed eval shard 0 at user 2007 (7d095e3; n=5000 finish gate caught it). Tuner baseline (5001-5200 subset): ahead 0.294204 imm 0.270881. Next: hp_tuner_5k loop.

change: BUDGET A/B: champion recipe at HALF budget -- WS 1 ep (6554 steps) + 0.25 ep decay (1638), everything else identical to champ5k_r1 (champion HPs, q72u learnable cbs)
note: vs champ5k_r1 paired on the same 5000 users: ahead -0.000058 (p=0.31, indistinguishable), imm +0.000430 BETTER (p=6.1e-62). The 2nd WS epoch (same 5000 users reshuffled) adds NOTHING -- consistent with the data-variety-beats-repetition lesson. ADOPTED (Andrew's rule): WS 1 ep + ratio-0.25 decay is now the budget for ALL 5k runs (tuner trials AND research runs); champion runs drop ~7h -> ~3.5h. SIZE/SPEED-class accept (efficiency budget, p-gate exempt; imm improvement is a bonus). Promoted: ckpt champ5kb1d_1638.pth + its learned cbs; 6554-step WS trace = the new prune ref. Wall-clock: WS 2h27m, decay 37m, eval 89m (2-sharded).

change: HP-TUNED config from hp_tuner_5k coordinate descent (20 trials incl. re-probes, tune-eval 5001-5200): weight_decay 0.01->0.2 + dropout_scale 1.0->0.5, all other coords settled AT champion values (peak_lr 1e-3, warmup 200, clip 0.25, decay_ratio 0.25, adamw_beta2 0.999, cb_lr_mult 1.0); standard 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs
status: REJECTED (worse both modes at full eval)
note: SUBSET-OVERFIT LESSON: the tuned config WON the 200-user tune-eval by +0.000762 ahead / +0.000978 imm vs the tuner baseline, with real-looking paired signal inside the subset (wd 0.1->0.2 imm p=5e-8 on those 200 users) -- and still INVERTED at n=5000 (paired vs champ5k_b1: ahead -0.000545, imm -0.000677, p=1.0 both). Per-user pairing within a subset cannot see subset-vs-population mismatch; 200 users cannot resolve sub-0.001 HP effects. CONCLUSIONS: (1) champ5k_b1 REMAINS champion, its HPs (wd 0.01, dropout 1.0) are confirmed vs 19 alternatives spanning peak_lr/warmup/wd/clip/decay_ratio/beta2/dropout/cb_lr; (2) any future sub-0.001 tuner verdict needs full-eval confirmation before adoption; (3) HP tuning is CLOSED for this phase -- next lever = state-size ladders (deck/preset/global). Descent detail in tuner_5k_log.jsonl; clean grid landscapes: wd peaked mid-grid on the subset (0.05 worst, 0.2 best), beta2 monotone to 0.999, dropout extremes (0, 2x) clearly bad, cb_lr_mult 0.1/10 both bad. Wall-clock ~4.6h (WS 2h29m, decay 37m, sharded eval 92m). Validation prune ran clean all tuning: 1 crash-bug fixed (Tensor sidecar), 0 kills fired (joint-AND held vs single-mode transients, incl. cb_lr_mult=10 imm-only breach) -- honest evals throughout.

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER deck rung 1: deck stream n_heads 2->1 at fixed d_model=32 (RWKV_STREAM_HEADS=deck:1) -- deck per-entity WKV state 2304->4352 floats (1.89x) at 264 FEWER params (per-head k/v_scale_linear heads shrink); card/note state unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs
note: Doubling the deck stream's recurrent memory for free (params actually -264) does NOT help: paired vs champ5k_b1 ahead -0.000271 / imm -0.000238, p=1.0 both -- within ~1 sigma of zero, so read as NO EFFECT rather than harm. The deck stream is not state-capacity-limited at d=32/H=2, and the 2x-bigger single-head WKV memory buys nothing (weak evidence that 2 independent heads >= 1 double-size head at equal d_model). Val trajectory tracked the champion within noise the whole WS (deltas -0.0021..+0.0031, no prune strikes). Deck knob of the ladder CLOSED (rung 1 gained <0.0003); per Andrew's order next knob = preset, but the same H-lever mechanism just failed on deck, so the better-motivated free rung is user/global H=1 (long-recurrence streams are the state-sensitive ones per the blanket-quant lesson) -- preset first per the specified order. Deploy note: rejected change would have grown per-deck deploy state 9->17 KB fp32 with zero accuracy return. Wall-clock 4.6h (WS 2h25m @ 1.43s/step -- deck K=32 costs ~18% step time, decay 36m, sharded eval 91m). Infra banked: RWKV_STREAM_HEADS / RWKV_STREAM_LAYERS per-stream arch hooks (commit d6fca68).

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER preset rung 1 (iter 5): preset stream n_heads 2->1 at fixed d_model=32 (RWKV_STREAM_HEADS=preset:1) -- preset per-entity WKV state 1728->3264 floats (1.89x, cap 10x) at 198 fewer params; card/note/deck unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs
note: Same verdict as the deck rung (iter 4): paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2) ahead -0.000215 / imm -0.000445, p=1.0 both -- no effect to slightly worse. The stronger prior (preset is long-recurrence: ~whole-history sequences, the state-sensitive class per the blanket-quant lesson) did NOT materialize: doubling the preset stream's recurrent memory buys nothing. H=1 free-state mechanism now 0/2 across stream types; last knob = user/global H=1 (iter 6), then the ladder closes and the >=50-iteration research phase begins (layer-adds move to the research phase as capacity questions -- no H-rung showed a state signal). OPS INCIDENT: the 2-parallel-shard eval WEDGED (both shards 50-85+ min on mega-users, 11.5/12 GB VRAM = WDDM oversubscription from the preset-K=32 chunk-state buffers, ~+0.8 GB/shard on 1M-token batches; 100% GPU util while 10-50x slow). Fixed via get_result per-shard resume run SEQUENTIALLY then eval_sharded relaunch-skip-merge (run_lad_preset1_evalfix.cmd, commit 5338c49); sequential shards ~47 min per shard remainder, no thrash at 3.75 GB. RULE: elevated-VRAM rungs (K=32 streams) use sequential shards. Wall-clock: WS 2h33m, decay 38m, eval (wedged 2h + sequential 1h37m).

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER user rung 1 (iter 6): user stream n_heads 2->1 at fixed d_model=32 (RWKV_STREAM_HEADS=user:1) -- user per-entity WKV state 1728->3264 floats (1.89x, cap ~50x) at 198 fewer params; card/note/deck/preset unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs, sequential-shard eval
note: THE FIRST LADDER RUNG WITH A REAL SIGNAL. Paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2): ahead +0.000345 (clears >=0.0003) p=1.3e-20, imm +0.000258 p=1.5e-29 -- BOTH modes improve with overwhelming significance, but imm misses the magnitude bar by 0.000042 -> strict gate = REJECT. Contrast deck (iter 4) and preset (iter 5): p=1.0, no effect at all. So the state-sensitivity prior was RIGHT about which stream matters (user = longest recurrence; it also sank blanket state-quant) even though H=1 alone did not clear the gate. Validation during WS tracked the champion within noise the whole run (the val set is only 10 users -- too small to see a 0.0003 effect). Per research-conduct rule 2 (near-miss -> variant), NEXT = iter 7 lad_user2: user H=1 + user layers 3->4 (RWKV_STREAM_LAYERS=user:4) -- user state 3264->4352 floats (2.5x baseline), params ~204k <= 225k cap -- pushing the same knob harder. Wall-clock: WS 2h26m, decay 37m, sequential eval 2h24m (~5.5h total), no incidents (sequential-shard rule worked; shard 0 71 min, shard 1 71 min).

change: STATE-SIZE LADDER user rung 2 (iter 7): iter-6 recipe (user n_heads 2->1) + user layers 3->4 (RWKV_STREAM_LAYERS=user:4) -- user per-entity WKV state 3264->4352 floats (2.52x champion), params 193,526->203,928 (+10.4k = one d=32 layer); card/note/deck/preset unchanged; champion recipe/HPs, 1-ep budget, q72u learnable cbs, sequential-shard eval
note: A MODE TRADE, not a win: paired vs champ5k_b1 (iter 2) ahead -0.000299 (p=1.0) / imm +0.000604 (p=7.8e-143). vs iter 6 (user H=1 alone): ahead -0.000643, imm +0.000346 -- the 4th user layer strongly boosts immediate-mode calibration but REGRESSES the ahead (cold forgetting-curve) mode. Combined attribution across the two rungs: state doubling (H=1, params ~flat) -> +ahead strongly +imm weakly; depth (+10.4k params + more state) -> +imm strongly -ahead. Neither variant clears BOTH bars. NEXT (iter 8): the lesson-bank SEED-PAIR DOCTRINE applies to iter 6's 0.000042 imm miss (thin-margin verdicts need the exact recipe re-run at a second RWKV_AUGMENT_SEED) -> lad_user1b = exact iter-6 recipe at seed 4321. If it clears both bars -> 1/2 seeds pass = Andrew's judgment call; same pattern -> reject stands (real but sub-bar; revisit if any later change adds imm); null -> iter 6 was partly seed luck. Wall-clock iter 7: WS 2h35m, decay 38m, sequential eval 2h26m (~5.7h), no incidents.
